_meta.description: EDCM Behavioral layer marker inventories for the 9-metric vector (C,R,D,N,L,O,F,E,I). Each metric section declares what markers enable, what requires structural detection, and what requires embeddings.
_meta.attribution: Claude generated; context, prompt Erin Spencer.
notes: Behavioral-EDCM is round-native, computed on CLOSED rounds.
Markers are lexical/structural cues that identify constraint-bearing spans.
Some metrics are fully computable from markers + structure. Some require embeddings. This is stated explicitly per metric.
Markers are case-insensitive. Multi-word markers use the same smash-join convention as bones_words_v1.
Marker lists are v1 starting points. They will grow from transcript testing.
The behavioral layer is acknowledged to have semantic dependencies that the operator layer avoids. This is intentional tensioning, not a defect.
hmmm: F (Fixation) and I (Integration Failure) require embeddings — no marker-only path exists for v1
D (Deflection) is a ratio that needs both constraint-relevant and constraint-irrelevant span detection — marker lists help but don't fully solve
whether safety/crisis markers (self-harm, violence) belong here or in a separate safety layer
actor attribution matters: 'I refuse' from speaker A vs speaker B has different diagnostic meaning. Markers fire per-turn; behavioral metrics aggregate per-round.

C.explanation: Full contradiction detection requires comparing semantic content across turns to find reversals. Markers identify explicit contradiction signals but miss implicit ones.

explicit_contradiction: you can't have it both ways
structural_signals: Negation of previously affirmed content (requires tracking across turns)
Same speaker asserting X then not-X within window
Rising P-family bone density in operator layer may correlate (bridge signal)

R.explanation: Both numerator (refusals) and denominator (constraint statements) are detectable from markers. This is the most marker-tractable metric.

D.explanation: Identifying what tokens are 'about constraints' requires understanding what the active constraints are. Markers identify deflection moves but the ratio requires constraint-topic tracking.

evasion: I don't want to talk about
I don't see how that matters

N.explanation: Resolution markers are detectable. Constraint tokens require span identification. The ratio is computable if constraint spans are identified first.

noise_indicators: Repetition of previously stated content without new information
Performative elaboration that adds length without resolving constraints
Hedging spirals that circle without landing
These require structural detection (comparing new tokens vs repeated tokens within window)

L.explanation: Count of tokens within constraint-bearing spans. Constraint spans identified by constraint_statement and request markers from R, plus these additional load indicators.

markers.notes: L is primarily a volume metric. High L means many constraint tokens in the window, regardless of whether they're resolved. It interacts with N: high L + low N = system generating volume without resolution.

O.formula: expansion beyond original scope per window
O.explanation: Detecting scope expansion requires knowing what the original scope was. Markers identify scope-expanding moves but the baseline scope must be tracked structurally or semantically.

scope_expansion: if we're doing that we might as well

F.explanation: Fixation measures semantic similarity between consecutive constraint expressions. This is intrinsically an embedding operation. No marker-only path exists. Under degraded conditions (no embedding model available), F must be disabled per canon: disable rather than invert.

structural_proxy: Repeated identical or near-identical phrases across turns (string-level detection)
Token overlap ratio between constraint spans in consecutive rounds
These are coarse proxies, not replacements for embedding similarity

E.explanation: Escalation is the positive derivative of commitment intensity. Detectable by tracking commitment markers across time and measuring whether they intensify.

escalation_signals: Transition from low → medium → high commitment markers across turns
Increasing use of modal verbs (must, will) vs hedges (might, could)
Rising P-bone density in operator layer may correlate (bridge signal)
Increasing sentence-final ! punctuation (P-bone from punct rules)

I.explanation: Full integration failure detection requires comparing what was corrected against what changed in the response. Markers identify correction events and acknowledgment, but measuring whether the correction was actually integrated requires semantic comparison.

integration_failure_signals: Acknowledgment followed by repetition of corrected behavior
Surface paraphrase of correction without structural change in subsequent output
Reversion to pre-correction behavior after one turn
These require multi-turn tracking, not single-marker detection
